info.title: VIN Decoder
info.description: Break down vehicle identification number into vehicle specification

get.description: VIN Decode Info returns an object with decode array of strings. Each string represents a 'variable' label that is available for given VIN when you do a 'VIN Decode' request. See variable labels that are returned here. This request is free.

parameters.name: CONTROL_SUM
parameters.description:
The CONTROL_SUM for this endpoint is generated by taking the first 10 characters of the SHA1 hash of your VIN, the operation ID 'info', API key, and secret key, concatenated in that order.
For instance: $controlSum = substr(sha1("{$vin}|info|{$apiKey}|{$secretKey}"), 0, 10);
For a complete guide, please refer to the How to create a request.

200.description: A successful response returns an object with decode array of strings. Each string represents a 'variable' label that is available for given VIN when you do a 'VIN Decode' request. See the VIN Decode Variable Labels List for a detailed list of variables, their data types, and examples.

Success response.value: {"price":0,"price_currency":"USD","decode":["VIN","Vehicle ID","Make","Manufacturer","Plant Country","Product Type","Manufacturer Address","Check Digit","Valid Check Digit","Check Digit Validity","Sequential Number","Body","Engine Cylinders","Engine Displacement (ccm)","Number of Doors","Engine Model","Engine Power (kW)","Fuel Type - Primary","Model","Model Year","Plant City","Series","Transmission","Engine (full)","Number of Seats","Engine Cylinders Position","Drive","Length (mm)","Height (mm)","Width (mm)","Fuel System","Engine Torque (RPM)","Wheelbase (mm)","Engine Position","Fuel Capacity (l)","Front Breaks","Rear Breaks","Valves per Cylinder","Track Front (mm)","Track Rear (mm)","Max Speed (km/h)","ABS","Wheel Size","Acceleration 0-100 km/h (s)","Engine Compression Ratio","Steering Type","Engine Stroke (mm)","Engine Cylinder Bore (mm)","Minimum Trunk Capacity (l)","Max Weight (kg)","Production Stopped","Production Started","Front Suspension","Rear Suspension","Fuel Consumption Urban (l/100km)","Fuel Consumption Extra Urban (l/100km)","Fuel Consumption Combined (l/100km)","Number of Gears","Valve Train","Power Steering","Maximum Trunk Capacity (l)","Wheel Rims Size","Engine Turbine","Minimum Turning Circle (m)","Emission Standard","CO2 Emission (g/km)","Drag Coefficient","Permitted trailer load without brakes (kg)","Width including mirrors (mm)","Max roof load (kg)","Permitted towbar download (kg)","Rear Overhang (mm)","Front Overhang (mm)","Permitted trailer load with brakes 8% (kg)","Engine Oil Capacity (l)","Engine Coolant (l)","Engine Max Speed (rpm)","Wheelbase Array (mm)","Wheel Size Array","Wheel Rims Size Array","Price","Odometer (km)","Make Logo"]}

When you reach a limit of requests per m.value: {"error":true,"message":"You've reached limit of 60 requests per minute. Contact us at info@vincario.com for a customized limit."}

When VIN can't be decoded.description:
##### **char**: VIN contains an invalid character (I, O, Q)

##### **length**: VIN length is not 17 characters

##### **invalid**: VIN is not valid

404.description: The route could not be found.

get.description: VIN Decode returns an object with price, balance and decode array of objects with 'label' and 'value' properties. Each decode object represents a 'variable'. See variable labels that are returned here.

parameters.name: CONTROL_SUM
parameters.description:
The CONTROL_SUM for this endpoint is generated by taking the first 10 characters of the SHA1 hash of your VIN, the operation ID 'decode', API key, and secret key, concatenated in that order.
For instance: $controlSum = substr(sha1("{$vin}|decode|{$apiKey}|{$secretKey}"), 0, 10);
For a complete guide, please refer to the How to create a request.

200.description: A successful response includes three main components: "price" and "price_currency" indicate the cost of the request, "balance" details remaining credits for various API services, and "decode" presents a detailed list of vehicle attributes. Each attribute in the decode array is represented by a label and its associated value, offering in-depth vehicle information. See the VIN Decode Variable Labels List for a detailed list of variables, their data types, and examples.

schema.description:
{
  "price": float,
  "price_currency": string,
  "balance": {
    "API Decode": integer,
    "API Stolen Check": integer,
    "API Vehicle Market Value": integer,
    "API OEM VIN Lookup": integer
  },
  "decode": [
    {
        "label": string,
        "value": string|integer|float|array
    },
    ...
  ]
}

##### **invalid**: VIN is not valid

get.description: The OEM VIN Lookup service performs an API query to the manufacturer's database and returns an object containing all available information about the vehicle. This includes vehicle basic and specific equipment, color codes, KBA numbers, specifications for electric vehicles, battery size, battery charging type, the original price of the vehicle, and much more.

parameters.name: CONTROL_SUM
parameters.description:
The CONTROL_SUM for this endpoint is generated by taking the first 10 characters of the SHA1 hash of your VIN, the operation ID 'oem', API key, and secret key, concatenated in that order.
For instance: $controlSum = substr(sha1("{$vin}|oem|{$apiKey}|{$secretKey}"), 0, 10);
For a complete guide, please refer to the How to create a request.

200.description: Returns an object containing all available information about the vehicle. This includes vehicle basic and specific equipment, color codes, KBA numbers, specifications for electric vehicles, battery size, battery charging type, the original price of the vehicle, and much more.

schema.description:
{
  "vin": string,
  "price": float,
  "price_currency": string,
  "balance": {
    "API Decode": integer,
    "API Stolen Check": integer,
    "API Vehicle Market Value": integer,
    "API OEM VIN Lookup": integer
  },
  "decode": [
    {
      "label": string,
      "value": mixed
    },
    {
      "label": string,
      "value": mixed
    },
    {
      "label": string,
      "value": mixed
    }
  ]
}

404.description: The route could not be found.

get.description:
Vehicle Market Value API returns an object with a vehicle details and its market value based on the last 365 days market data. You'll be able to evaluate the vehicle market price and average odometer reading based on given VIN.
Response is returned when at least 10 records of similar vehicle is available on the market, otherwise the request is not charged.

parameters.name: CONTROL_SUM
parameters.description:
The CONTROL_SUM for this endpoint is generated by taking the first 10 characters of the SHA1 hash of your VIN, the operation ID 'vehicle-market-value', API key, and secret key, concatenated in that order.
For instance: $controlSum = substr(sha1("{$vin}|vehicle-market-value|{$apiKey}|{$secretKey}"), 0, 10);
For a complete guide, please refer to the How to create a request.

parameters.name: odometer
parameters.description: Odometer reading used for price adjusted calculation. When provided, the response will include a price_odometer_adjusted field with an estimated price based on the provided odometer reading.

parameters.name: odometer_unit
parameters.description: Unit of measurement for the odometer reading. Valid values are 'km' (kilometers) or 'mi' (miles). Defaults to 'km' if not provided.

200.description: Returns an object containing all available information about the vehicle. This includes vehicle basic and specific equipment, color codes, KBA numbers, specifications for electric vehicles, battery size, battery charging type, the original price of the vehicle, and much more.

schema.description:
{
  "vin": string,
  "price": float,
  "price_currency": string,
  "balance": {
    "API Decode": integer,

      "odometer_stdev": integer

  "records": [
    {
      "market": string|null,

      "odometer": integer,

      "market": string|null,
      "continent": string|null,
      "price": integer,
      "price_currency": string,
      "odometer": integer,
      "odometer_unit": string
    }
  ]
}

get.description: VIN Stolen Check returns an object with price, balance and stolen array of objects with 'code' and 'status' properties. Each stolen object represents a 'database'.

parameters.name: CONTROL_SUM
parameters.description:
The CONTROL_SUM for this endpoint is generated by taking the first 10 characters of the SHA1 hash of your VIN, the operation ID 'stolen-check', API key, and secret key, concatenated in that order.
For instance: $controlSum = substr(sha1("{$vin}|stolen-check|{$apiKey}|{$secretKey}"), 0, 10);
For a complete guide, please refer to the How to create a request.

200.description: Returns an object with price, balance and stolen array of objects with 'code' and 'status' properties. Each stolen object represents a 'database'.

schema.description:
{
  "vin": string,
  "price": float,
  "price_currency": string,
  "balance": {
    "API Decode": integer,
    "API Stolen Check": integer,
    "API Vehicle Market Value": integer,
    "API OEM VIN Lookup": integer
  },
  "stolen": [
    {
        "code": string,
        "status": "stolen"|"not-stolen"|"could-not-verify"
    },
    ...
  ],
  "make_logo": string|null
}

Success response.description:
Code could have these values:

  • cz - Czech Republic
  • hu - Hungary
  • ro - Romania
  • si - Slovenia
  • sk - Slovakia
  • it - Italy
  • vincario - Our own database of stolen cars

Status could have these values:

  • stolen - VIN is reported as stolen in the police database.
  • not-stolen - VIN is NOT reported in the police database.
  • could-not-verify - Respective police database failed to respond. Please, try again later.
Success response.value: {"vin":"XXXDEF1GH23456789","price":0,"price_currency":"CZK","balance":{"API Decode":218,"API Stolen Check":205,"API Vehicle Market Value":205,"API OEM VIN Lookup":0},"stolen":[{"code":"cz","status":"not-stolen"},{"code":"sk","status":"stolen"},{"code":"ro","status":"could-not-verify"}],"make_logo":"https://vindecoder.eu/img/make/volvo.svg"}

When you reach a limit of requests per m.value: {"error":true,"message":"You've reached limit of 60 requests per minute. Contact us at info@vincario.com for a customized limit."}
